factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, particularly in Commercial Airplanes, driven by higher spending on program development.

evidence.text: Research and development expense increased by $480 million and $196 million during the nine and three months ended September 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The increase in expense was primarily due to the 777X program at BCA.

factors.summary: Labor relations are deteriorating due to the ongoing work stoppage by IAM 751, which is causing significant production disruptions.

evidence.text: The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers District 751 (IAM 751) has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models [...] The IAM 751 work stoppage is also significantly reducing aircraft deliveries and adversely impacting our financial position, results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: No evidence suggests the company is raising or holding prices; focus is on operational issues.

evidence.text: The higher net loss of $8.0 billion during the nine months ended September 30, 2024, compared to $2.2 billion during the same period in 2023 primarily reflects higher losses from operations at BCA and BDS.

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to increase, signaling cautious growth expectations.

evidence.text: We continue to expect capital expenditures in 2024 to be higher than in 2023.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing, with total debt rising to $57.7 billion.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2024, the total debt balance was $57.7 billion, up from $52.3 billion at December 31, 2023.

factors.summary: No evidence of share buybacks or dividend changes; focus is on liquidity.

evidence.text: We had $10.0 billion of cash, $0.5 billion of short-term investments, and $10.0 billion of unused borrowing capacity on revolving credit line agreements.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is negative, indicating cash outflows.

evidence.text: Net cash used by operating activities was $8.6 billion during the nine months ended September 30, 2024.

factors.summary: An acquisition of Spirit is underway, which could create value but also poses risks.

evidence.text: On June 30, 2024, we entered into an agreement to acquire Spirit in an all-stock transaction at an equity value of approximately $4.7 billion.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is uncertain due to labor issues and economic challenges.

evidence.text: We expect to be able to access capital markets when we require additional funding in order to pay off existing debt, address further impacts to our business related to the strike by IAM 751, a delay in our ability to resume production and ramp up production and deliveries, or market developments, fund outstanding financing commitments or meet other business requirements.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are increasing, particularly in commercial airplanes due to lower deliveries.

evidence.text: The growth in Inventories was primarily driven by lower deliveries on our commercial airplane programs during the nine months ended September 30, 2024 as compared to the same period in 2023.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance costs are not a significant factor in the provided text.

evidence.text: No specific information on environmental regulations or compliance costs is provided.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind due to labor strikes and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).

factors.summary: Interest rate increases are raising borrowing costs and affecting liquidity.

evidence.text: Moody’s downgraded our short term and long term credit ratings to Baa3/P-3 in April 2024. Moody's and S&P placed our ratings on review for downgrade in September 2024 and October 2024, respectively.

factors.summary: FX movements have had a minor impact, as evidenced by a small effect on cash and cash equivalents.

evidence.text: Effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents 8   (22)

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed organic revenue growth, with an increase in BDS revenues offset by a significant decrease in BCA revenues due to lower deliveries and production disruptions. Additionally, production pauses and supply chain constraints are contributing to the deceleration in organic revenue growth.

evidence.text: BDS revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2024 increased by $320 million compared with the same period in 2023.

BCA revenues decreased by $5,321 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2024, compared with the same period in 2023 driven by lower deliveries across all programs and 737-9 customer considerations related to the January 2024 grounding.

Commercial airplane cash outflows reflect slowed and/or paused production and lower deliveries as a result of ongoing safety and quality improvement actions the Company is taking following the Alaska Airlines accident on January 5, 2024, supply chain constraints, and the ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751.

factors.summary: The order backlog has shown a decrease, primarily driven by the Commercial Airplanes segment. This reduction is attributed to paused production, delivery delays, and increased cancellations, despite some new orders.

evidence.text: BCA total backlog decreased from $440,507 million as of December 31, 2023, to $427,733 million at September 30, 2024, reflecting an increase in the value of existing orders that, in our assessment, do not meet the accounting requirements of ASC 606 for inclusion in backlog and cancellations, partially offset by new orders in excess of deliveries.
The decrease in contractual backlog during the nine months ended September 30, 2024, was primarily due to a decrease in BCA backlog.

Commercial airplane cash outflows reflect slowed and/or paused production and lower deliveries as a result of ongoing safety and quality improvement actions the Company is taking following the Alaska Airlines accident on January 5, 2024, supply chain constraints, and the ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751.

factors.summary: The overall trend in unit volumes is negative, with declines observed across multiple segments. While the Accounting Quantity section mentions growing unit volumes and higher production rates, the Revenues and Cash Flow Summary subsections indicate a decline, particularly in Commercial Airplanes, due to production pauses, delivery delays, and operational challenges.

evidence.text: Cumulative firm orders represent the cumulative number of commercial jet aircraft deliveries plus undelivered firm orders. Firm orders include certain military derivative aircraft that are not included in program accounting quantities.

Commercial airplane deliveries, including intercompany deliveries, were as follows: [...] Deliveries during the first nine months of 2024 229  (4)   15  (7) 11  36  291  Deliveries during the first nine months of 2023 286  (6) 1  17  (6) 17  50  371  [...] Deliveries during the third quarter of 2024 92  (2) 6  (2) 4  14  116  Deliveries during the third quarter of 2023 70  (1)   8  (5) 8  19  105

Commercial airplane cash outflows reflect slowed and/or paused production and lower deliveries as a result of ongoing safety and quality improvement actions the Company is taking following the Alaska Airlines accident on January 5, 2024, supply chain constraints, and the ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751.

factors.summary: Labor costs are negatively impacting margins due to a work stoppage by IAM 751, which has paused production and increased abnormal production costs. The disruptions are affecting financial position, results of operations, and cash flows, with significant reductions in aircraft deliveries.

evidence.text: Production has been paused since September 13, 2024, as a result of the ongoing work stoppage initiated by IAM 751.
The inspections and rework costs on inventoried aircraft are accounted for as abnormal production costs, and we expensed $209 million in the nine months ended September 30, 2024.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers District 751 (IAM 751) has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models [...] The IAM 751 work stoppage is also significantly reducing aircraft deliveries and adversely impacting our financial position, results of operations and cash flows.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).

factors.summary: Margins are under significant pressure, primarily driven by unfavorable contract adjustments, production inefficiencies, higher costs, and disruptions in key segments like Commercial Airplanes and BDS. These factors are leading to compression in both gross and operating margins.

evidence.text: Net unfavorable cumulative contract catch-up adjustments for the nine months ended September 30, 2024 were $258 million higher than the prior year comparable period.

Cost of sales as a percentage of Revenues increased during the nine months ended September 30, 2024, compared with the same period in 2023, primarily due to the reach-forward losses on the 777X and 767 programs, lower margins at BCA, and higher charges on BDS fixed-price development programs.

The higher net loss of $8.0 billion during the nine months ended September 30, 2024, compared to $2.2 billion during the same period in 2023 primarily reflects higher losses from operations at BCA and BDS.

factors.summary: Program execution is encountering significant challenges across multiple major programs, including delays, cost overruns, and technical issues, particularly affecting the 737 MAX, 777X, and T-7A Red Hawk programs. Additionally, labor strikes and safety concerns have exacerbated these issues, further impacting production and execution.

evidence.text: The FAA found multiple instances where the companies failed to comply with manufacturing quality control requirements and provided 90 days from March 1, 2024, to submit a corrective action plan. [...] We recorded a $2.6 billion reach-forward loss on the 777X program in the third quarter of 2024.

During the third quarter of 2024, we recorded a reach-forward loss of $908 million on the T-7A Red Hawk program that was primarily driven by projected increases in supplier cost estimates. In addition, we recorded losses on the KC-46A Tanker and Commercial Crew programs that were partially attributable to higher supplier costs.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).

factors.summary: The U.S. government defense budgets are stable and support major defense programs, but there are risks of delays or reductions due to potential funding uncertainties and the need for Congress to approve appropriations bills or a continuing resolution to avoid a government shutdown.

evidence.text: The Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2024, and the Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2024, included funding for Boeing’s major programs, including P-8, CH-47 Chinook, F-15, KC-46A Tanker, AH-64 Apache, V-22 Osprey, and Space Launch System.

The company relies on the U.S. government in various aspects of its defense, commercial, and services businesses. Congress and the President must enact either full-year fiscal year 2025 (FY25) appropriations bills or an additional CR to fund government departments and agencies after December 20, 2024, or a government shutdown could result.

factors.summary: The supply chain health is facing significant challenges, including ongoing disruptions, inflationary pressures, and production inefficiencies. These issues are leading to higher costs, reduced productivity, and pauses in production.

evidence.text: We are continuing to experience factory disruption, including supply chain delays and quality issues. [...] Higher near-term production costs.

The company and its suppliers are experiencing inflationary pressures, as well as supply chain disruptions as a result of global supply chain constraints and labor instability. [...] These factors have reduced overall productivity, disrupted our operations and adversely impacted our financial position, results of operations and cash flows.

Commercial airplane cash outflows reflect slowed and/or paused production and lower deliveries as a result of ongoing safety and quality improvement actions the Company is taking following the Alaska Airlines accident on January 5, 2024, supply chain constraints, and the ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751.

factors.summary: The contract backlog is showing mixed trends. While the overall backlog increased, there are notable decreases in specific segments. The Commercial Airplanes segment experienced a decrease in backlog, whereas the Defense, Space & Security backlog grew. Additionally, production pauses and delivery delays have impacted the contract backlog.

evidence.text: BDS backlog of $61,621 million at September 30, 2024 compared with $59,012 million as of December 31, 2023, reflects the timing of awards, partially offset by revenue recognized on contracts awarded in prior periods.

The decrease in contractual backlog during the nine months ended September 30, 2024, was primarily due to a decrease in BCA backlog. [...] The decrease in unobligated backlog during the nine months ended September 30, 2024, was primarily due to reclassifications to contractual backlog at BDS.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).

factors.summary: Demand for commercial airplanes is slowing, with evidence pointing to lower revenues, production pauses, supply chain issues, and work stoppages.

evidence.text: BCA revenues decreased by $5,321 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2024, compared with the same period in 2023 driven by lower deliveries across all programs and 737-9 customer considerations related to the January 2024 grounding.

Commercial airplane cash outflows reflect slowed and/or paused production and lower deliveries as a result of ongoing safety and quality improvement actions the Company is taking following the Alaska Airlines accident on January 5, 2024, supply chain constraints, and the ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing due to production slowdowns, quality issues, paused production, and labor strikes. The company is adjusting production rates in response to supplier constraints and enhanced maintenance processes, leading to reduced aircraft deliveries and financial losses.

evidence.text: Our planned production rates are dependent on our suppliers' ability to support our operations and our ability to meet heightened quality control requirements.
The company is slowing production and implementing enhanced maintenance processes, which have led to reduced aircraft deliveries and financial losses.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, primarily impacting Commercial Airplanes and negatively affecting overall margins. This shift is driven by operational disruptions, production issues, and increased expenses.

evidence.text: BCA loss from operations increased by $4,203 million reflecting reach-forward losses on 777X and 767, lower deliveries, lower margins driven by production disruption including the IAM 751 work stoppage, 737-9 customer considerations related to the January 2024 grounding and higher research and development expense, partially offset by lower abnormal production costs.
The change in Non-cash items is primarily due to the 777X and 767 reach-forward losses of $3.0 billion recorded in the third quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum is mixed. While the Defense, Space & Security segment is growing, the Commercial Airplanes segment is facing challenges due to production disruptions and lower deliveries. Additionally, defense and space production has been paused due to a work stoppage, which may further impact these segments.

evidence.text: BDS revenues increased by $320 million primarily due to higher volume on weapons and proprietary programs, partially offset by higher net unfavorable cumulative catch-up adjustments on major fixed-price development programs.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction programs, including slowing production and enhanced maintenance processes, but these efforts have not yet yielded significant savings and have negatively impacted financial results due to ongoing disruptions.

evidence.text: The company is taking actions including slowing production and implementing enhanced maintenance processes, but these have significantly impacted financial results.
The change in Non-cash items is primarily due to the 777X and 767 reach-forward losses of $3.0 billion recorded in the third quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, supply chain disruptions, and labor instability, negatively impacting margins and operational efficiency. Additionally, production pauses and work stoppages are exacerbating these cost increases.

evidence.text: The company is experiencing inflationary pressures, as well as supply chain disruptions as a result of global supply chain constraints and labor instability. [...] During the third quarter of 2024, we recorded a reach-forward loss of $908 million on the T-7A Red Hawk program that was primarily driven by projected increases in supplier cost estimates.

Commercial airplane cash outflows reflect slowed and/or paused production and lower deliveries as a result of ongoing safety and quality improvement actions the Company is taking following the Alaska Airlines accident on January 5, 2024, supply chain constraints, and the ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751.

factors.summary: The impact of trade tariffs is mixed. While U.S.-China trade relations are significant and could affect commercial aircraft sales and supply chains, there is no specific mention of trade tariffs' direct impact on cash flow in the provided information.

evidence.text: The current state of U.S.-China relations remains an ongoing watch item. China is a significant market for commercial aircraft and we have long-standing relationships with our Chinese customers, who represent a key component of our commercial aircraft backlog. Overall, the U.S.-China trade relationship remains stalled as economic and national security concerns continue to be a challenge.
No information on trade tariffs or their impact is provided in the text.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges due to regulatory changes, particularly in the form of enhanced FAA inspections and aircraft groundings, which have resulted in increased costs and operational difficulties. However, the cash flow summary indicates that these changes are not significantly impacting the company's operations, suggesting a potential inconsistency in the information provided.

evidence.text: Following the accident, the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) grounded and required inspections of all 737-9 aircraft with a mid-exexit door plug [...] The FAA approved an enhanced maintenance and inspection process that was required to be performed on each of the grounded 737-9 aircraft.
No specific regulatory changes are mentioned that are affecting the company's operations.

factors.summary: The company has significant litigation exposure, including a pending fine with the U.S. Department of Justice and various ongoing legal proceedings, which could result in substantial financial and operational impacts.

evidence.text: Eliminations and other unallocated items expense for the nine and three months ended September 30, 2024, increased by $349 million and $99 million compared with the same periods in 2023. The increase during the year includes a second quarter earnings charge of $244 million that reflects a fine that would be paid if an agreement with the U.S. Department of Justice is approved by the federal district court.
Legal Various legal proceedings, claims and investigations are pending against us. Legal contingencies are discussed in Note 18 to our Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements.

factors.summary: The company faces significant near-term risks, primarily from ongoing labor stoppages, supply chain disruptions, and regulatory challenges, which could further impact production and financial results. Additionally, prolonged labor strikes and supply chain issues pose ongoing threats to operations.

evidence.text: The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers District 751 (IAM 751) has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models...

factors.summary: Management is exhibiting caution due to various challenges including labor strikes, supply chain disruptions, and economic uncertainties, which have impacted production and financial performance. The ongoing work stoppage and production pauses across multiple aircraft models highlight the significant operational and financial strain the company is currently facing. These factors collectively indicate a negative sentiment in management's confidence as they navigate through these disruptions.

evidence.text: The company is facing significant challenges from the IAM 751 work stoppage, supply chain disruptions, and regulatory actions, which have led to reduced production and financial losses.

The ongoing work stoppage initiated on September 13, 2024, by IAM 751 has paused production of certain commercial aircraft models (737, 767, 777 and 777X aircraft) as well as production of commercial derivative aircraft for our Defense, Space & Security business (KC-46A Tanker and P-8A Poseidon).
